values.value:
== RECORD TYPE RULES == Record Types are embedded inside the CustomObject XML as <recordTypes> blocks. metadataType: CustomObject memberName: ObjectName or ObjectName__c
CRITICAL: Standard objects use NO __c suffix: Case, Account, Contact, Lead, Opportunity
ELEMENT ORDER inside <recordTypes> MUST be: 1. fullName 2. active 3. businessProcess (ONLY for Case/Lead/Opportunity/Solution — see below) 4. description (optional) 5. label 6. picklistValues (optional)
-- RECORD TYPE XML -- <recordTypes> <fullName>Record_Type_API_Name</fullName> <active>true</active> <description>Optional description</description> <label>Record Type Label</label> <picklistValues> <picklist>Status__c</picklist> <values> <fullName>Active</fullName> <default>false</default> </values> <values> <fullName>Inactive</fullName> <default>true</default> </values> </picklistValues> </recordTypes>
CRITICAL: Inside <values>, ONLY two elements allowed: <fullName> and <default> NEVER include <label> inside <values>.
== PROCESS-DRIVEN OBJECTS — CRITICAL == Case, Lead, Opportunity, and Solution record types REQUIRE a <businessProcess> element. Omitting it causes: "Required field is missing: businessProcess" Object → Business Process: Case→Support Process, Lead→Lead Process, Opportunity→Sales Process ALWAYS use the first entry from orgState.existingBusinessProcesses for the relevant object.
CORRECT XML for a Case record type: <recordTypes> <fullName>Technical_Support</fullName> <active>true</active> <businessProcess>Basic Support Process</businessProcess> <label>Technical Support</label> </recordTypes>
== STANDARD OBJECT RECORD TYPES — ABSOLUTE RULES == When the object is a STANDARD object: 1. MINIMAL CustomObject XML — ONLY <recordTypes> blocks inside <CustomObject>. NOTHING else. FORBIDDEN: <label>, <pluralLabel>, <nameField>, <sharingModel>, <deploymentStatus> 2. NEVER generate a CustomTab file for a standard object. 3. NEVER add <tabVisibilities> in any Profile XML for a standard object. 4. NEVER generate a Layout file for a standard object.
QUICK REFERENCE for standard-object record type (e.g. Account): File 1: objects/Account.object — CustomObject with ONLY <recordTypes> block(s) File 2: profiles/Admin.profile — Profile with <layoutAssignments> + <recordTypeVisibilities> NO CustomTab. NO <tabVisibilities>. NO Layout file. Ever.
-- PROFILE ASSIGNMENT FOR RECORD TYPES -- <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <Profile xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <layoutAssignments> <layout>ObjectName-Layout Name</layout> <recordType>ObjectName.RecordTypeDeveloperName</recordType> </layoutAssignments> <recordTypeVisibilities> <recordType>ObjectName.RecordTypeDeveloperName</recordType> <default>false</default> <visible>true</visible> </recordTypeVisibilities> </Profile>
LAYOUT REFERENCE FORMAT: ObjectName-Layout Name (one dash). Example: Account-Account Layout HOW TO FIND STANDARD OBJECT LAYOUT NAME: use orgState.standardObjectLayoutNames["Account"][0]
DEFAULT RECORD TYPE RULE — CRITICAL: Salesforce requires exactly ONE recordTypeVisibility per object to have default=true. - If ticket says Default=true → set <default>true</default> - If ticket says Default=false but it is the only record type → STILL set <default>true</default> - NEVER emit <recordType>ObjectName.Master</recordType> — Master is not a valid Metadata API name - NEVER leave all recordTypeVisibilities with default=false
-- PICKLIST VALUES -- 1. Only include <picklistValues> if ticket specifies which values to assign 2. If ticket says "all values" → include ALL picklist values from existingFields in orgState 3. If ticket does not mention picklist values → OMIT <picklistValues> entirely 4. NEVER fabricate picklist values — only use values that exist in orgState
-- RULES SUMMARY -- 1. ALWAYS preserve ALL existing record types when updating — never drop any 2. For new object + record type → chained deployment 3. For existing object + record type → single deployment 4. NEVER emit two files[] entries with same filePath — merge into one
Record Type XML, Profile assignment, picklist values, process-driven objects
